name: GameAssist
description:
**GameAssist is a modular D&D 5E (2014) automation suite for Roll20. It brings critical misses, condition guidance, concentration checks, token controls, NPC hit points, encounter history, configuration, and diagnostics together without forcing every campaign to use every feature.**

GameAssist v0.1.5.0 includes eight individually configurable modules supported by a shared, toggleable MarkerService. Guided menus and plain-language help make the common workflows approachable, while direct commands and deeper controls remain available when a campaign needs them.

---

## ⚙️ Requirements

- **Roll20 access:** Mod scripts require a game created by a Roll20 Pro subscriber. Players in that game do not need their own Pro subscriptions.
- **Character sheet:** D&D 5E (2014 Character Sheet)
- **Integrated services:** Remove standalone TokenMod and StatusInfo before normal v0.1.5.0 use. TokenAssist and ConditionAssist provide the supported integrated replacements, and MarkerService owns their shared marker behavior.
- **NPC tokens:** NPCManager and NPCHPRoller use token bar 1 for HP. Tokens must be on the Objects layer, linked to a character with `npc=1`, and need `npc_hpformula` when GameAssist rolls their HP.
- **CritFumble tables:** `CF-Melee`, `CF-Ranged`, `CF-Thrown`, `CF-Spell`, `CF-Natural`, `Confirm-Crit-Martial`, and `Confirm-Crit-Magic`

> GameAssist does not invent your campaign's fumble results. Create and populate the seven rollable tables before using CritFumble table rolls.

> TokenAssist uses `!token-assist` and `!ta`/`!ta-*` commands. Older supported `!token-mod` macros continue temporarily during v0.1.x and should be updated before v0.2.0. See the README for the supported command list.

---

## 🚀 Quick Start

GameAssist is controlled from Roll20 chat. Commands beginning with `!` are sent to Mods instead of appearing as ordinary chat messages; players do not install anything separately.

1. From the game's **Settings**, open **Mod (API) Scripts** and add **GameAssist** from the One-Click library.
2. Remove standalone TokenMod and StatusInfo so overlapping commands and marker listeners are not loaded twice.
3. Create the seven CritFumble rollable tables if you plan to use CritFumble.
4. Allow the Mod sandbox to restart, then type `!ga-status` in chat to check the installation.
5. Open `!ga-config ui` and enable only the modules your campaign needs.

Use `!token-assist help`, `!condition help`, `!critfumble help`, `!concentration --help`, or `!npc-death-report --help` whenever you want an in-game guide.

## 🧩 Included Modules

- **CritFumble** — Watches supported attack templates for natural 1s, offers the attacker clear choices, and rolls from your melee, ranged, thrown, spell, or natural-attack fumble tables. The GM can also open a player picker manually or roll martial and magic confirmation tables directly.
- **ConditionAssist** — Uses 2014 SRD condition wording by default, offers optional 2024 SRD wording and campaign edits, and provides case-insensitive `!cond-<condition>` quick references, marker artwork, a selected-token condition menu, and verified marker-toggling announcements in public chat or player whispers. Valid legacy StatusInfo definitions can be copied without deleting the rollback state.
- **TokenAssist** — Provides `!token-assist` and `!ta`/`!ta-*` token controls for selected or authorized tokens. It handles common visibility settings, names, tooltips, bars, auras, colors, layers, position, size, rotation, vision, lighting, links, controllers, relative values, movement, stacking order, reports, and built-in or custom status markers. Marker changes always pass through MarkerService.
- **ConcentrationTracker** — Builds normal, advantage, or disadvantage Constitution saves from damage, remembers each player's latest check, manages a concentration marker through MarkerService, and lists concentrating tokens on the page marked by Roll20's Player Ribbon.
- **NPCManager** — Marks defeated NPCs, records revivals, checks HP against death markers, and organizes encounter history into readable handouts using **Campaign > Chapter > Section > Session** scopes. Custom Arc rosters can follow NPCs connected to a character story, vow, rivalry, investigation, redemption, or any other campaign thread.
- **NPCHPRoller** — Rolls `npc_hpformula` for selected NPCs or qualifying NPCs on the page marked by Roll20's Player Ribbon. Optional automatic rolling handles newly added tokens while setup protection prevents placeholder HP from producing false death and revival records.
- **ConfigUI** — Provides a GM-only chat interface for enabling modules, changing common settings, reviewing configuration, and reading module summaries. Command-based configuration remains available for advanced users.
- **DebugTools** — Provides optional GM-only damage, marker, and saving-throw diagnostics. DebugTools starts disabled, and every action remains a preview until the GM includes `--apply`.

---

## 🎲 Example at the Table

A linked NPC falls from positive HP to 0. NPCManager records that death in the active Campaign, Chapter, Section, and Session scopes, then asks MarkerService to apply the configured death marker. If the NPC later rises above 0 HP, GameAssist records the revival and removes the marker through the same service.

The GM can review a recent summary, audit the current page, write polished records to campaign handouts, or collect selected NPCs into a custom Arc roster. When a token needs an adjustment outside those automated workflows, TokenAssist can change the selected token without introducing a second marker system.

## 💬 Main Commands

### System and Configuration

- `!ga-status` — Show overall health, module state, errors, and dependency information
- `!ga-status --details` — Add activity, listener, timing, marker-service, and overlap diagnostics
- `!ga-config ui` or `!ga-config-ui` — Open the GM configuration panel
- `!ga-config modules` — List feature modules, core services, and their current status
- `!ga-config list` — Write a versioned configuration-only snapshot handout
- `!ga-config get <ModuleOrService> [key]` / `!ga-config set <ModuleOrService> <key>=<value>` — Review or change component configuration
- `!ga-enable <ModuleOrService>` / `!ga-disable <ModuleOrService>` — Enable or disable a feature module or core service
- `!ga-metrics` — Show GameAssist runtime metrics

### Critical Misses

- `!critfumble help` — Open the CritFumble quick-reference guide
- `!critfumble menu` — Open the guided Natural 1 menu
- `!critfail` — Open the direct manual player picker
- `!critfumble-<melee|ranged|thrown|spell|natural>` — Roll the selected fumble table
- `!confirm-crit-martial` / `!confirm-crit-magic` — Roll confirmation tables

### Conditions

- `!condition` — Open the selected-token condition menu
- `!condition help` — Open the quick-start guide
- `!condition <name>` or `!cond-<condition>` — Show one configured condition description
- `!condition add|remove|toggle <condition...>` — Change condition markers on selected tokens when permitted
- `!condition announce`, `!c-a`, or `!cond-!` — Toggle a condition marker, then report who is or is no longer affected
- `!condition config` — Choose 2014 SRD, 2024 SRD, or campaign-custom wording and open definitions or validated import/export

### Token Controls

- `!token-assist help` or `!ta-help` — Open the TokenAssist quick guide
- `!token-assist about` or `!ta-about` — Review attribution, supported behavior, and current limits
- `!token-assist config` or `!ta-config` — Manage explicit player-ID targeting
- `!ta-on|off|flip <property...>` — Change supported token switches
- `!ta-set <property|value...>` — Change supported token values, auras, or status markers
- `!ta-move`, `!ta-order`, or `!ta-report` — Move, stack, or report on targeted tokens
- `!ta-ids <token-or-character-id...>` — Target explicit IDs when authorized
- `!token-assist --<action> ...` — Use the same actions with the full command name
- `!token-mod ...` — Older supported syntax; replace in campaign macros before v0.2.0

### Concentration

- `!concentration` or `!cc` — Open concentration-check buttons
- `!concentration --damage <amount> --mode <normal|adv|dis>` — Roll a concentration save
- `!concentration --last` — Repeat the player's previous check
- `!concentration --off` — Remove the configured marker from selected linked tokens
- `!concentration --status` — List concentrating tokens on the page marked by Roll20's Player Ribbon
- `!ga-conc-status` — Show the GM concentration activity summary

### NPC History and Reporting

- `!npc-death-report --help` or `!npc-death-help` — Open the central NPCManager guide
- `!npc-death-report [--scope campaign|chapter|section|session] [--recent|--page N|--write]` — Review a reporting scope or open the report writer
- `!npc-death-buckets` — Review or rename active reporting scopes
- `!npc-death-write` or `!npc-wr` — Write NPC history to handouts
- `!npc-death-audit` — Check HP and death-marker consistency and update the audit handout
- `!npc-death-arc` — Create and manage custom Arc rosters from selected tokens or the current Session
- `!npc-death-clear [--scope campaign|chapter|section|session] [--nested] [--confirm]` — Clear records using explicit confirmation

### NPC Hit Points and Diagnostics

- `!npc-hp-selected` — Roll HP for selected qualifying NPCs
- `!npc-hp-all` — Roll HP for qualifying NPCs on the page marked by Roll20's Player Ribbon
- `!ga-debug` — Open diagnostics after enabling DebugTools

> GameAssist help panels explain available choices in plain language. The full README contains the complete command matrix, permissions, examples, module settings, report workflows, migration guidance, and troubleshooting procedures.

---

## 🔄 Working Alongside Other Mods

GameAssist is designed to share a campaign with unrelated Roll20 Mods. Problems arise when two enabled tools respond to the same command, control the same token property, or react to the same event.

- **General token controls:** TokenAssist uses `!token-assist` and `!ta`/`!ta-*`. Older `!token-mod` syntax is left to standalone TokenMod when that script is detected. Remove standalone TokenMod for normal v0.1.5.0 use because both tools can still change the same token properties and markers.
- **Conditions:** Standalone StatusInfo overlaps ConditionAssist's `!condition` commands and marker descriptions. Remove it when ConditionAssist is enabled.
- **NPC HP in token bar 1:** Another Mod that automatically rolls, replaces, damages, heals, or synchronizes bar 1 can trigger GameAssist's HP and death-history workflows. Choose one tool to own automatic NPC HP changes, or disable the overlapping feature.
- **Status markers:** Another Mod that automatically changes the same death, concentration, or condition marker can compete with GameAssist and produce confusing results. Assign one automation tool to each marker.
- **Natural 1 attack handling:** Another Mod that watches the same attack templates for natural 1s can produce duplicate menus or multiple fumble results. Keep only one automatic critical-miss workflow enabled.
- **GameAssist report handouts:** Report, audit, Arc, and configuration handouts are regenerated from GameAssist data when their write commands run. Keep unrelated campaign notes in separate handouts so they are not replaced.

These cautions concern overlapping behavior, not the mere presence of another Mod. Lighting, initiative, maps, macros, character utilities, and unrelated automation can continue normally.

---

## 🛡️ Safeguards You Can See

- Newly added NPC tokens receive a short setup window so placeholder HP does not create a false death and revival.
- TokenAssist validates a command before changing token properties and refuses unsupported features without applying the other requested changes.
- Every TokenAssist status operation uses MarkerService, including built-in, numbered, custom-name, and exact-tag markers.
- Built-in and custom markers are matched to the exact marker Roll20 stores on the token, and ConditionAssist displays marker artwork when Roll20 exposes it.
- `!npc-death-audit` creates or updates a dedicated handout listing NPCs that need attention.
- Every DebugTools action is a dry run until the GM includes `--apply`.
- MarkerService can be disabled when another Mod must own marker behavior. GameAssist first disables ConditionAssist, TokenAssist, NPCManager, ConcentrationTracker, and DebugTools, while CritFumble, ConfigUI, and NPCHPRoller remain available.

## 🩺 Troubleshooting

Start with `!ga-status`, then use `!ga-status --details` and `!ga-config modules`. Open the affected module's help panel before changing persistent state or enabling DebugTools.

> MarkerService should appear enabled when marker-dependent modules are in use. Test a real death, concentration marker, condition cycle, and harmless selected-token command on disposable tokens before relying on a new installation.

## 📚 Documentation and Support

See the [GameAssist README](https://github.com/Mord-Eagle/GameAssist#readme) for complete installation, configuration, command syntax, examples, permissions, report workflows, migration notes, troubleshooting, and upgrade guidance.

Bug reports and feature requests are welcome through [GameAssist Issues](https://github.com/Mord-Eagle/GameAssist/issues).
conflicts: Other scripts that modify NPC HP or use bar1_value
Other scripts that automatically add or remove the same death or concentration status markers
Other scripts that automatically process natural 1 attack rolls or open critical-fumble menus
commands: !token-mod <older supported macro syntax; replace before v0.2.0>
